title: Allergic rhinitis

presenting_history.text: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped allergic-rhinitis terminology, rhinorrhoea, sneezing, postnasal drip, smell, eye symptoms, triggers, timing, and selected nasal fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

red_flags.text: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped epistaxis, fever, facial-pain, purulent-discharge, headache, unilateral, and visual warning fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

investigations.text: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-selected allergy-history or testing context without autonomous testing.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

investigations.text: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-documented allergen, nasal-care, medicine-plan, return, question, and follow-up discussions without doses.
investigations.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.

source_limitations: Detailed nasal-mucosa, throat, ear, mastoid, hearing, tonsil, lymph-node, neck, shiner, facial-swelling, previous-record, fixed two-to-four-week, and combined history-draft fields remain unsupported.
Allergic rhinitis remains clinician-stated and symptoms must not be used to infer infection or structural disease.
Medicine choice or dose, autonomous treatment, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
